_readme: All document_text values simulate raw OCR output from scanned invoices. Numbers are intentionally correct/incorrect per test case spec — errors are in the source document, not OCR noise (except TC06 which tests heavy garbling).

test_cases.name: Happy Path — Good OCR, All Math Correct
test_cases.description: Typed invoice, good scan quality. Minor OCR artefacts: 'l' for 'I', extra spaces in headers. All 3 validation checks pass.

test_cases.name: Line Item Math Error — qty × unit_price ≠ amount
test_cases.description: OCR quality is fine. The source document itself contains a typo: Ergonomic Laptop Stand printed as $125.00 instead of $135.00 (3 × $45.00). Subtotal and total are internally consistent with the wrong amount so only line_item_0 check fires.
test_cases.math: item0: 3×45=135≠125(FAIL). item1: 2×30=60. item2: 4×15=60. item3: 1×55=55. SubtotalCheck: 125+60+60+55=300=reported(OK). TotalCheck: 300+30=330(OK).

test_cases.name: Subtotal Mismatch — Σ line_items ≠ subtotal
test_cases.description: All 4 line items have correct per-item arithmetic. Sum of amounts = 3,210.00 but the supplier printed Net Amount = 2,500.00. Tax and total are internally consistent with the wrong subtotal. Only Check 2 (subtotal sum) fails.

test_cases.name: Sparse Invoice — No invoice_number, No Line Item Detail
test_cases.description: Scan of a handwritten-style informal invoice. No invoice number, no structured line items with quantity/unit_price, no tax line. Validator skips all null fields. Status COMPLETED with many null fields.

expected_validation.note: All checks skipped — null values are not failures per validator logic

test_cases.name: Heavy OCR Garbling — Character Substitutions Throughout
test_cases.description: Poor quality scan with heavy OCR noise: | for l/I, 0 for O, + for t, 1 for i. All underlying numbers are arithmetically correct. Tests LLM ability to normalise OCR noise and still extract valid data.

test_cases.name: Zero Tax Invoice — tax_total = 0.00
test_cases.description: Canadian freelancer invoice with explicitly zero tax. Tests that tax=0 makes Check 3 pass (0 is a valid value, not null — validator should not skip it).

expected_validation.note: Check 3 uses 0.00 — should pass, not be skipped

test_cases.name: Non-Invoice Document — Meeting Notes
test_cases.description: A meeting notes document accidentally submitted to the invoice endpoint. No invoice structure at all. LLM should return all nulls. No validation checks run (no line items, no amounts). Expected: FAILED or NEEDS_REVIEW terminal state.

expected_validation.note: No checks run — extraction yields all nulls, zero line items

test_cases.document_text:
Meeting Notes - Product Team Standup
November 15 , 2024

Attendees : Alice , Bob , Carlos , Diana

Discussion Points :
1. Sprint 47 retrospective - team agreed to reduce WlP limits
2. New feature deployment scheduled for next Tuesday
3. Coffee machine on floor 3 needs repair - facilities notified
4. Holiday party planning : venue shortlisted to 3 options

Action ltems :
- Alice : Send out survey for holiday party venue by Friday
- Bob : Follow up with facilities about coffee machine
- Carlos : Update sprint board with new WlP limits

Next standup : Monday 9am
Notes recorded by Diana

test_cases.name: Duplicate Invoice — Same invoice_number, Different Document Text
test_cases.description: Different OCR text but same invoice_number INV-2024-0847 as TC01. Simulates supplier re-sending a copy. Tests duplicate detection by invoice_number. IMPORTANT: submit TC01 first.

expected_behavior.note: Service should detect INV-2024-0847 already processed and return duplicate=true. Document your behaviour in README.

test_cases.name: Idempotency Retry — Exact Same document_text as TC01
test_cases.description: Identical document_text to TC01. Simulates client retry after timeout (never received first response). Tests content-hash deduplication. Service should NOT process twice. IMPORTANT: submit TC01 first.

expected_behavior.note: Depending on implementation: same request_id returned, or duplicate=true with existing result, or new request. Document your choice and justify it in README.

test_cases.name: Multiple Simultaneous Failures — line_item AND total Both Wrong
test_cases.description: Two validation failures at once: Steel Reinforcing Bars 5×$80=$400 but printed as $350 (line_item_1 fails). Subtotal is consistent with wrong amount so subtotal check passes. But printed total is $700 vs correct $660 (total fails). Tests that retry prompt contains BOTH failures.

test_cases.name: Large Invoice — 10 Line Items, All Correct
test_cases.description: 10-item invoice. Tests that all per-item checks loop correctly and subtotal aggregation works at scale. All math correct. Expect 12 checks total (10 line items + subtotal + total).

submission_order: TC01 (baseline — must run before TC11 and TC12)
TC02, TC03, TC04, TC13 (validation failure cases — can run in any order)
TC05, TC07, TC08, TC09 (edge cases — any order)
TC11 (duplicate by invoice_number — requires TC01 submitted first)
TC12 (idempotency — requires TC01 submitted first, use identical document_text)
